UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS IRMA ENCINAS, ANDRES ALVARADO AND SAUL ALVARADO'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND: (1) FRAUD WITH PARTICULARITY; AND (2) INTENTIONAL NUISANCE. STOIBER V. HONEYCHUCK (1980) 101 CAL.APP.3D 903. TO THE EXTENT PLAINTIFF ALLEGES NEGLIGENT NUISANCE IT IS DUPLICATIVE OF THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE AND DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. VAN ZYL V. SPIELBERG (1969) 2 CAL.APP.3D 367; EL ESCORIAL OWNER'S ASS'N V. DLC PLASTERING (2007) 154 CAL.APP.4TH 1337. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: LILLIAN K. SING; CLERK L. FONG; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (D501)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Nov-24-2014 Assigned To Trial Court

JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Nov-24-2014 Text Ruling

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-28-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances on Thursday, August 28, 2014:
Mary Catherine Wiederhold, Esq. (415.533.0735) appearing for Plaintiffs Marla Fisher, Natalee, Oona Hanawalt, and B. Alison Panko;
No other appearances.

Plaintiffs submit to the Court's tentative ruling. Counsel also submits a proposed order. RULING - The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS IRMA ENCINAS, ANDRES ALVARADO, and SAUL ALVARADO'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Paragraphs 20, 28, 31, 26, and 57 are stricken with leave to amend to allege facts supporting malice, oppression or fraud. Paragraphs 9 and 10 are stricken with leave to amend to allege facts linking these allegations to Plaintiffs' causes of action. Motion is DENIED as to paragraph 69. Restitution (labeled as disgorgement in the complaint) is an available remedy per Korea Supply v. Lockhead (2003) 29 Cal.4th 1134. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Lillian K. Sing; Clerk L. Fong; matter not reported. (D501)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-28-2014 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances on Thursday, August 28, 2014:
Mary Catherine Wiederhold, Esq. (415.533.0735) appearing for Plaintiffs Marla Fisher, Natalee, Oona Hanawalt, and B. Alison Panko;
No other appearances.

Plaintiffs submit to the Court's tentative ruling. Counsel also submits a proposed order.
DEFENDANTs IRMA ENCINAS, ANDRES ALVARADO and SAUL ALVARADO's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED with leave to amend: (1) fraud with particularity; and (2) intentional nuisance. Stoiber v. Honeychuck (1980) 101 Cal.App.3d 903. To the extent Plaintiff alleges negligent nuisance it is duplicative of the cause of action for negligence and demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Van Zyl v. Spielberg (1969) 2 Cal.App.3d 367; El Escorial Owner's Ass'n v. DLC Plastering (2007) 154 Cal.App.4th 1337. Order signed in open court. Judge: Lillian K. Sing; Clerk L. Fong; matter not reported. (D501)
